validation.slugMinLength: Slug must be at least 2 characters

validation.nameMinLength: Name must be at least 2 characters
validation.slugMinLength: Slug must be at least 2 characters
validation.slugPattern: Only lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens

stepRegionalConfig.title: Regional settings
stepRegionalConfig.subtitle: We detected your preferences — confirm or adjust
stepRegionalConfig.localeHint: Automatically sets currency, country and language
stepRegionalConfig.timezoneHint: Defines how "today" and "this week" are interpreted in reports

stepFunnelConfig.title: Configure funnel
stepFunnelConfig.subtitle: Define the steps you want to track
stepFunnelConfig.validationMinStep: Add at least 1 step to the funnel

stepApiKey.title: API key + Installation
stepApiKey.subtitle: Paste a script on your site and data arrives automatically
stepApiKey.generateErrorToast: Error generating API key. Try again.
stepApiKey.keyWarning: This key authenticates all events for {orgName}. Do not commit to Git — use an environment variable (NEXT_PUBLIC_GROWARE_KEY).
stepApiKey.snippetHint: Paste in the <head> of any page. The tracker captures UTMs, device, referrer and pageviews automatically.
stepApiKey.identifyDescription: After login, link the anonymous session to the user profile. This enables per-customer attribution, session history and behavioral analysis by segment.
stepApiKey.stepsConfigured: {count, plural, =1 {# step configured} other {# steps configured}}
stepApiKey.noStepsConfigured: No funnel steps configured. Go back to the previous step to define your funnel.

optionalEvent.title: Optional event: checkout_started
optionalEvent.description: If your flow has a checkout (modal, redirect or separate page), add this event before purchase. It enables checkout abandonment analysis — how many users got here but didn't convert. The tracker detects abandonment automatically via beforeunload.

stepInstallTracker.title: Install tracker
stepInstallTracker.subtitle: API key, snippet and real-time verification
stepInstallTracker.sdkGridDescription: Select your project's framework or language to see a personalized installation tutorial.

tutorial.title: Integrate with {sdk}
tutorial.descriptionWeb: Install the tracker in your project and start capturing events automatically.
tutorial.descriptionServer: Send server-side events via HTTP POST to the Groware API.
tutorial.aiPromptTitle: Use our prompt and let AI do everything
tutorial.aiPromptDescription: Paste this prompt in your AI assistant (Cursor, ChatGPT, Copilot) and it will handle the entire integration automatically.
tutorial.docsHint: For more details on events and properties, check the

common.addEnvVarDesc: Store your API key as an environment variable. Never hardcode it in source files.
common.createHookDesc: A typed hook that wraps window.Groware safely. It uses requestIdleCallback so tracking never blocks user interactions.
common.identifyUsersDesc: Call identify() right after the user authenticates to link their anonymous session to their profile. Call reset() on logout.
common.trackFunnelEventsDesc: Call track() at each step of your funnel. Place these calls in the exact server actions or client components where each action happens.
common.enableDebugDesc: Add data-debug='true' to see all Groware events logged in the browser console. Remove before deploying to production.
common.validateInstallDesc: Test that events are arriving correctly before going live.
common.calloutAutoCaptureBody: The tracker automatically captures: pageview on every route change (App Router compatible), UTMs from the URL, device type, referrer, and session ID. No extra code needed for these.
common.calloutIdentifyWarning: Important: never use email as customer_id
common.calloutIdentifyWarningBody: customer_id must be your internal UUID. Email, CPF, or name must never be used as identifiers. Pass them to identify() as traits instead.
common.calloutDebugBody: Open DevTools → Console and look for [Groware] logs. You'll see each event as it fires, including auto-captured pageviews and UTM data.
common.calloutValidateChecklistBody:
1. Open your site with ?utm_source=test&utm_medium=cpc in the URL
2. Open DevTools → Network → filter by '/api/track'
3. Navigate pages — you should see POST requests returning 204
4. Complete a funnel action (signup, checkout, etc.)
5. Come back to this onboarding page — the verification card below will show 'Event detected!'
common.trackClientEventsDesc: Use the hook created in the previous step to fire events at each key funnel step, directly inside your components.
common.trackServerEventsDesc: For payment events, always use server-side via fetch POST to ensure the event is never blocked by the browser and is processed exactly once with dedupe_id.
common.calloutClientVsServerBody: Use track() client-side for UI events (signup, checkout_started, page_view). Use server-side fetch for financial events (purchase, subscription) — this ensures that even with AdBlock or JS errors, payments are still recorded.
common.calloutResetOnLogoutBody: Always call reset() when the user logs out. This clears the anonymous session and prevents future events from being attributed to the previous user.
common.bindServiceProviderDesc: Register GrowareService as a singleton so it's shared across the request lifecycle.

nextjs.installScriptDesc: Add the script to your root layout using next/script with strategy afterInteractive. This ensures the tracker loads after the page is interactive without blocking rendering.
nextjs.createHookDesc: Create a typed hook that wraps window.Groware safely. It uses requestIdleCallback so tracking never blocks user interactions.
nextjs.identifyUsersDesc: Call identify() right after the user authenticates to link their anonymous session to their profile. Call reset() on logout.
nextjs.trackClientEventsDesc: Use the useTracker hook in your Client Components to fire events at each funnel step. Place track() calls at the exact moment the action happens.
nextjs.trackFunnelEventsDesc: For payment events, always use server-side via fetch POST. Never rely on client-side payment events — they can be blocked by AdBlock or fail silently.

react.installScriptDesc: Add the script tag to your index.html before the closing body tag. For Vite, reference the env var as a data attribute using a script in main.tsx instead.
react.envVarDesc: Store your API key as an environment variable. For Vite-based React projects use VITE_ prefix; for Create React App use REACT_APP_.
react.createHookDesc: A typed React hook that safely wraps window.Groware with requestIdleCallback to avoid blocking the main thread.
react.identifyUsersDesc: Call identify() when the user logs in to link the anonymous session to their profile.
react.trackClientEventsDesc: Use the useTracker hook in your components to fire client-side events at each funnel step: signup, checkout_started, and other UI-triggered events.
react.trackFunnelEventsDesc: For purchases, send the event server-side in your payment webhook. Financial events should always come from the server to avoid AdBlock blocking.
react.calloutViteEnvDesc: If you need the key from env, load it dynamically in main.tsx: document.querySelector('script[data-groware]')?.setAttribute('data-key', import.meta.env.VITE_GROWARE_KEY)

html.installScriptDesc: Paste this script in the <head> of every page. Load it early to capture UTMs from the initial URL.
html.calloutAutoPageLoadBody: pageview, utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, referrer, device (mobile/desktop), and session ID are captured automatically with no extra code.
html.identifyUsersDesc: Call this right after your login logic completes. On logout, call reset() to clear the session.
html.trackPurchaseDesc: Fire this event when a purchase is confirmed. The dedupe field prevents double-counting if the page is reloaded.
html.trackOtherFunnelDesc: Fire these at each key step of your funnel.
html.enableDebugDesc: Replace your script tag with the debug version to log events in the console.

vue.createComposableDesc: A Vue 3 composable that wraps window.Groware safely for use across your components.
vue.identifyUsersDesc: Call identify() in the Pinia store login action.
vue.trackFunnelEventsDesc: Use the composable in your components to fire client-side events: signup after registration, checkout_started when checkout opens, and purchase on confirmation.

angular.createServiceDesc: An injectable Angular service that wraps window.Groware. Inject it anywhere in your app.
angular.identifyUsersDesc: Inject GrowareService into AuthService and call identify after login.
angular.trackFunnelEventsDesc: Inject GrowareService into your components and call track on funnel events: signup, checkout_started, and purchase.

svelte.createUtilDesc: A typed Svelte module that safely wraps window.Groware. Import and use anywhere in your app.
svelte.trackFunnelEventsDesc: Use the module in your routes to fire client-side events: signup after registration, checkout_started when the checkout page loads, and purchase on confirmation.

wordpress.installScriptDesc: This is the recommended method. Add it to your theme's functions.php or a custom plugin. The tracker will load on all pages.
wordpress.calloutPluginBody: You can also use a header/footer injection plugin like 'Insert Headers and Footers' to paste the script tag without touching code.
wordpress.identifyUsersDesc: If users are logged in, identify them after the tracker loads so their events are attributed.
wordpress.calloutUserIdBody: Pass $user->ID (WordPress integer ID), never the email address, as the first argument to identify().
wordpress.trackWooDesc: Hook into WooCommerce's thank you page to fire the purchase event.
wordpress.enableDebugDesc: Temporarily add data-debug='true' to see events in the browser console. Remove before going live.

gtm.createTagDesc: In GTM: Tags → New → Tag Configuration → Custom HTML. Paste the script below. Set trigger to 'All Pages'.
gtm.calloutTagOrderBody: Set this tag to fire before all other tags (Tag Sequencing). This ensures UTMs from the URL are captured before any other tag runs.
gtm.configureTriggerDesc: Set the trigger to 'All Pages - Page View' so the tracker loads on every page immediately.
gtm.calloutTriggerSettingsBody: Go to Triggering → All Pages. This fires the tracker on every page load and SPA navigation captured by GTM.
gtm.createEventTagTitle: Create a Custom Event Tag for purchases
gtm.createEventTagDesc: Create a separate Custom HTML Tag triggered by a custom event (e.g. 'groware_purchase') pushed to dataLayer from your app.
gtm.pushFromAppDesc: In your frontend code, push to dataLayer to trigger the GTM tags.
gtm.createVariablesDesc: Create Data Layer Variables for each field used in the tags: DL - Invoice ID, DL - Order Total, DL - Customer ID, DL - Product ID.
gtm.calloutVariablesBody: Variables → New → Data Layer Variable. Set the name to match the dataLayer keys (e.g. 'invoiceId' for DL - Invoice ID). These map dataLayer values to your tag templates.
gtm.previewPublishDesc: Use GTM Preview mode to verify events fire correctly before publishing.
gtm.calloutPreviewBody:
1. Click Preview in GTM → enter your site URL
2. Navigate pages — verify the Groware tag fires on 'All Pages'
3. Complete a purchase — verify the purchase tag fires
4. Check DevTools Network for POST /api/track returning 204
5. Publish the container

server.addEnvVarDesc: Store your API key as an environment variable.
server.createModuleDesc: A reusable helper module so you don't repeat the fetch boilerplate everywhere. Place it in a shared utilities folder.
server.trackPurchaseDesc: Call in your payment webhook handler. Always use dedupe_id to prevent double-counting.
server.trackRecurringDesc: For SaaS with recurring revenue, track renewals and cancellations server-side from webhook handlers.

nodejs.createModuleDesc: A typed helper module so you don't repeat the fetch boilerplate everywhere. Place it in a shared utilities folder.

python.createModuleDesc: A reusable module using requests (sync) or httpx (async). Place in your project's utils or services directory.
python.asyncVariantDesc: For async frameworks like FastAPI or Django Async, use httpx instead of requests.

php.createModuleDesc: A reusable PHP class using cURL. Place it in your app's Services or Helpers directory.

go.createModuleDesc: A typed Go package that handles the HTTP request. Import it from any handler.

ruby.createModuleDesc: A reusable Ruby module. Place it in lib/ or app/services/ depending on your framework.

laravel.createServiceDesc: A typed Laravel service using Http facade. Register it in a service provider or use dependency injection.

stepChoosePath.title: How do you want to start?
stepChoosePath.subtitle: Choose the best way to connect your data
stepChoosePath.gatewayDescription: Paste your API key and see your dashboard populated automatically
stepChoosePath.trackerDescription: Install a script on your site to track UTMs, channels and conversions

stepGateway.title: Connect payment gateway
stepGateway.subtitle: Paste your API key to import data automatically
stepGateway.calloutDescription: The tracker captures UTMs, channel and funnel. The gateway brings real revenue. Together, you see how much each channel generated.
stepGateway.mercadoPagoDescription: Payments in Brazil via Mercado Pago
stepGateway.syncingSubtitle: We are syncing your payment history
stepGateway.syncFailed: Sync failed. You can try again in settings.

stepVerifyEvent.waitingForEvents: Waiting for events from the last 5 minutes...

verified.title: All set, {firstName}!
verified.subtitle: Events arriving. Your dashboard is ready to use.

unverified.title: Almost there, {firstName}!
unverified.nextStepDescription: Install the tracker in your project and come back to see your data. Use the prompt below to integrate with AI in seconds.
unverified.goBack: Go back and verify installation now
